name: Supply Chain AI
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: Supply chain intelligence engine — demand narrative interpretation, supplier risk assessment from unstructured reports, anomaly root-cause explanation, natural-language supply chain query interface, and scenario simulation narration for what-if planning

dev.note: ~80K tokens/day, prototype demand analysis and NL queries

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput, real-time global supply chain analysis

services.name: Azure Machine Learning
services.purpose: Demand forecasting and optimization models — time-series forecasting (ARIMA, Prophet, DeepAR), inventory optimization, lead-time prediction, supplier scoring models, and route optimization using managed endpoints for real-time inference

prod.note: Standard_DS4_v2 training + managed endpoint for inference

enterprise.note: NC6s_v3 GPU training, multi-model endpoints, MLflow registry

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Supply chain operational data store — real-time inventory levels, order tracking, supplier performance metrics, demand forecast results, logistics events, and cross-facility stock positions with global distribution and low-latency reads

enterprise.note: Multi-region writes, 500GB, global supply chain state

services.name: Azure Event Hubs
services.purpose: Real-time supply chain event ingestion — IoT sensor data (warehouse temperature, fleet GPS), POS transaction streams, supplier shipment notifications, logistics status updates, and demand signal capture at millions of events per second

services.name: Azure Stream Analytics
services.purpose: Real-time event processing — demand signal aggregation, inventory threshold alerting, logistics delay detection, supplier SLA monitoring, and windowed aggregations for near-real-time dashboards

services.name: Azure Blob Storage
services.purpose: Supply chain data lake — historical demand data, supplier performance archives, logistics records, ML training datasets, forecast result archives, and compliance documentation for audit

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: ML workspace secrets, OpenAI API keys, Event Hubs connection strings, Cosmos DB keys, supplier API credentials, and ERP integration tokens

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Platform observability — forecast accuracy tracking (MAPE/RMSE), inventory turnover rates, demand signal processing latency, model inference performance, and supply chain KPI dashboards

optimization_tips: Use Azure ML batch endpoints for non-urgent forecasts (weekly/monthly demand planning) instead of real-time managed endpoints — batch inference is 60-70% cheaper and ideal for scheduled forecast refreshes

Implement tiered storage lifecycle policies for supply chain data — hot storage for last 90 days, cool for 90 days-1 year, archive for 1+ years; most supply chain analytics only need recent data, saving 50-60% on storage

Use GPT-4o-mini for routine supply chain queries (order status, inventory levels, shipment tracking) and reserve GPT-4o for complex scenario analysis (what-if simulations, multi-factor root cause analysis) — 80% of queries are routine
Right-size Event Hubs throughput units to actual demand signal volume — start with 1 TU (1MB/s ingress) and enable auto-inflate rather than over-provisioning fixed capacity

Cache frequently accessed forecasts and inventory snapshots in Redis (15-minute TTL) — demand forecasts and stock levels change slowly relative to query frequency, reducing Cosmos DB RU consumption by 40%

Train ML models on sampled datasets for hyperparameter tuning (10-20% sample), then train the final model on full data — reduces GPU compute hours for experimentation by 80% without impacting final model quality
